comments.text: After discussion with Gemini Pro I got the following observation. A recent paper of Bailleul and Riblet appears to give a new partial result toward this problem which is not yet reflected on this page. They prove that every \(n\)-element subset of \(\mathbb{R}\) contains a Sidon subset of size at least\[ \left(\frac{1}{3\sqrt{3}}+o(1)\right)\sqrt{n}. \]Equivalently, in the notation of this problem,\[ \ell(n)\ge \left(\frac{1}{3\sqrt{3}}+o(1)\right)n^{1/2}. \]This does not settle the conjectural asymptotic\[ \ell(n)\sim n^{1/2}, \]nor the stronger Alon--Erdős Sidon partition conjecture, but it gives an explicit improved constant in the known lower bound. The result uses a compression lemma based on injective Freiman \(2\)-morphisms into cyclic groups, combined with Singer-type Sidon constructions. The paper is: J. Bailleul and O. Riblet, \emph{On the largest Sidon subset in a finite subset of \(\mathbb{R}^N\)}, arXiv:2605.03181. J. Bailleul and O. Riblet, On the largest Sidon subset in a finite subset of R<sup>N</sup>, arXiv:2605.03181 The Bailleul--Riblet method does not immediately solve the conjecture, but it suggests a useful reduction: better lower bounds for \(\ell(N)\) can be obtained by improving the quantitative compression step. In particular, any improvement in the ratio\[ \frac{|B|}{\sqrt m}, \]where \(B\) is the subset retained by the compression lemma and \(\phi:B\to \mathbb Z_m\) is an injective Freiman \(2\)-morphism, would give a new explicit lower-bound constant for \(\ell(N)\). An essentially lossless compression, with\[ |B|=(1-o(1))N \qquad\text{and}\qquad m=(1+o(1))N, \]would imply the conjectural asymptotic\[ \ell(N)\sim N^{1/2}. \]
